---
description: Create a structured PR with validation evidence and reviewer guidance
agent: PROrchestrator
---

# Create PR — Agent Prompt

Create a well-structured pull request that includes validation evidence, a clear intent summary, and guidance for human reviewers.

## Inputs

- **Target branch**: `{{ workflow.input.target_branch | default('main') }}`
- **Draft**: `{{ workflow.input.is_draft | default(false) }}`
- **Work items**: `{{ workflow.input.work_items | default('') }}`

{% if workflow.input.existing_pr is defined and workflow.input.existing_pr %}
### Resuming Existing PR
- PR URL: {{ workflow.input.existing_pr }}
{% endif %}

## ⛔ Out of Scope — DO NOT Do These

- **Do NOT run `dotnet build`, `dotnet test`, `npm test`, or any build/test commands.** Phase 3 (CI Watch) handles build validation. Your only job is to create the PR from already-committed code.
- **Do NOT validate code correctness.** Assume all code changes are already validated by prior phases.
- **Do NOT check or reference previous run results.** Each run is independent — use only the inputs provided to this workflow.

## Workflow

### 1. Gather Context

#### Detect CI Platform
```powershell
$scriptPath = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".copilot\installed-plugins\octane\octane-pr-orchestrator\skills\deterministic-scripts\scripts\detect-platform.py"
python $scriptPath
```
Use the JSON output (`platform`, `org`, `project`, `repo`) for all API calls. Do NOT parse the git URL yourself.

#### Get Branch Info
```bash
git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD    # Current (source) branch
git log {{ workflow.input.target_branch | default('main') }}..HEAD --oneline  # Commits to include
```

### 2. Load the PR Body Template

Read the PR body template from the installed plugins directory:

```powershell
$templatePath = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".copilot\installed-plugins\octane\octane-pr-orchestrator\skills\pr-templates\references\pr-body.md"
Get-Content $templatePath -Encoding UTF8
```

> ✅ **CHECKPOINT**: Before proceeding, confirm you have the template content with sections: `## Intent`, `## Changes`, `## Validation`, and the `DIGEST_LINK_PLACEHOLDER` line at the top. If the file wasn't found, try `Get-ChildItem -Path $env:USERPROFILE -Recurse -Filter "pr-body.md" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -First 1`. Do NOT improvise a different PR body format.

Here is the template structure for reference (use the file content as the authoritative source):

{% raw %}
```markdown
<!-- pr-orchestrator -->
# {{PR_TITLE}}

> 📋 **[Review Digest](DIGEST_LINK_PLACEHOLDER)** — Shows what requires human judgment, what was mechanically fixed by agents with evidence, and all automated gate results.

## Intent
> _What does this change accomplish and why?_
{{INTENT_SUMMARY}}

## Changes
> _Logical grouping of changes, not a file-by-file list._
{{CHANGE_GROUPS}}

## Validation
**Tests**: {{TESTS_RUN}} run, {{TESTS_PASSED}} passed, {{TESTS_FAILED}} failed
**Code Review**: {{CODE_REVIEW_SUMMARY_ONELINER}}

### Related Work Items
{{WORK_ITEMS}}

---
<sub>Generated by [PR Orchestrator](https://github.com/azure-core/octane) — Phase 2</sub>
```
{% endraw %}

### 3. Generate PR Content

Use the `pr-body.md` template as the structure for the PR description. Replace the `{% raw %}{{...}}{% endraw %}` placeholders with actual values.

#### Intent Summary
- Analyze commits, changed files, and the **actual code diffs** to understand the business logic change
- Write a detailed summary (3-5 sentences minimum) of **what** changed and **why**, focusing on the business impact:
  - What business problem or requirement does this address?
  - What behavior changed for users / callers / downstream systems?
  - What was the previous behavior, and what is it now?
- Do NOT just restate the commit message. Read the code changes and explain the substance.

#### Change Groups
- Group changes by logical business purpose, not by file
- For each group, explain **what** the change does in business terms (not just "updated X")
- Example: "Added environment injection to PowerShell command generation so test passes execute in the correct Cirrus environment" not "Modified StringUtils.ts and TestPassRunService.cs"

#### Validation Summary (Compact)
- Include compact one-liners:
  - `**Tests**: {run} run, {passed} passed, {failed} failed`
  - `**Code Review**: {summary of findings by severity}`
- **IMPORTANT**: The template has a `DIGEST_LINK_PLACEHOLDER` text in the Review Digest link at the top. Leave it **literally as-is** — Phase 4 will replace it with the actual digest comment URL after posting. Do NOT replace it with an empty string, a URL, or remove the line.

### 4. Generate PR Title

Follow conventional commit format when possible:
- If all commits share a type: `{type}({scope}): {summary}`
- If mixed: `{primary_type}: {summary of main change}`
- Keep under 72 characters
- Be specific: "Add JWT authentication to /api/users" not "Update code"

### 5. Create the Pull Request

> ⚠️ **Architecture**: The agent creates the PR with a **placeholder** description.
> A deterministic script (`apply-pr-body-template.py`) runs immediately after and sets
> the full body via REST API — this avoids all PowerShell encoding/mangling issues.
> You MUST still write the full body to the temp file so the script can read it.

**Step A — Write body to temp file** (the script reads this):
```powershell
$bodyFile = "$env:TEMP\pr-body.txt"
Set-Content -Path $bodyFile -Value $prBody -Encoding UTF8
Write-Host "PR body written to $bodyFile ($($prBody.Length) chars)"
```

**Step B — Create PR with placeholder description**:

**ADO:**
```powershell
az repos pr create `
  --title "{pr_title}" `
  --description "<!-- pr-orchestrator -->" `
  --source-branch {source_branch} `
  --target-branch {{ workflow.input.target_branch | default('main') }} `
  --org https://dev.azure.com/{org} `
  --project {project} `
  --repository {repo} `
  --output json
  # Add --draft if is_draft is true
  # Add --work-items {ids} if work_items are specified
```

If the PR already exists (TF401179 error), update the title only:
```powershell
az repos pr update --id {pr_id} --title "{pr_title}" --org https://dev.azure.com/{org} --detect false
```

**GitHub:**
```powershell
$bodyFile = "$env:TEMP/pr-body.txt"
gh pr create `
  --title "{pr_title}" `
  --body-file "$bodyFile" `
  --base {{ workflow.input.target_branch | default('main') }} `
  --head {source_branch} `
  --repo {owner}/{repo}
  # Add --draft if is_draft is true
```

> **Note**: GitHub's `gh pr create --body-file` works reliably. ADO's `az repos pr create --description`
> does NOT reliably handle multi-line content on Windows, hence the placeholder approach for ADO.

### 6. Report Result

```
✅ PR created successfully!

PR: {pr_url}
Title: {pr_title}
Source: {source_branch} → {{ workflow.input.target_branch | default('main') }}
Status: {active|draft}
Work Items: {linked_items}

Next: Run Phase 3 (watch-and-fix) to monitor the CI build.
```

> ⚠️ **If created as draft**: Warn that most CI pipelines do not trigger automatically on draft PRs.

## Expected Output

Populate these output fields:

- **pr_url**: Full URL of the created pull request
- **pr_title**: PR title (conventional commit format)
- **pr_status**: `draft` or `active`
- **source_branch**: Source branch name
- **target_branch**: Target branch name
- **work_items_linked**: List of work item IDs linked to the PR
